questions.text: A grandfather clock measures time by counting the number of oscillations of a small-angle pendulum. You take a grandfather clock that gives the correct time at sea level (where $g = 9.81 m/s^2$) up a mountain and notice that after two days it is running five minutes behind. What is the value of the gravitational acceleration up that mountain?

choices.e: There is not enough information to answer the question

questions.text: The Hubble space telescope, having a mass of 11 110 kg, is in low-Earth orbit with a semi-major axis of 6 924 km. What is its orbital period?
A 75 kg person gets in an elevator. How much more power must the elevator's motor supply if the elevator is to go up at the same constant speed of 0.5 m/s as it goes up when empty?

A 2 kg ball is initially stationary on a flat, frictionless surface. It is then hit by a 0.25 kg piece of putty traveling at 25 m/s. After the collision, the putty sticks to the ball. At what speed will the putty-covered ball travel?

You are holding a bottle of pop in your hands on a bus. The bubbles in the pop are going straight up. Suddenly, the bus brakes hard to avoid a road hazard. How is the motion of the bubbles in the pop affected?

choices.a: The bubbles will gain some motion towards the front of the bus.
choices.b: The answer depends on the initial speed of the bus.
choices.c: The motion of the bubbles will not change.
choices.d: The bubbles will gain some motion towards the back of the bus.

questions.text: Consider an open circuit consisting of a battery with voltage $V$, a resistor of resistance $R$ and a capacitor of capacitance $C$, all in series. Before closing the circuit, the capacitor carries no charge. After how long does the current running through the circuit drop by half compared to the moment it is closed?

Which value is closest to the average net speed of electrons ("drift speed") in a copper wire of radius 1 mm for a current of 3 Amperes? The density of copper at room temperature is about 9 g/cm³ and its molar mass is 63.5 g/mol.

Consider three charges located at the three vertices of an equilateral triangle of edge length $L$. Two of the charges have a value of $+Q$ and the third one has a value of $-2Q$. What is the magnitude of the net electric field at the centre of the triangle?
An FM radio station broadcasts signals at a frequency of about 100 MHz. What is approximately the wavelength of such a signal?

choices.e: More information is needed to answer this question

questions.text: A bright flash of light has a measured intensity of 800 W/m² at a distance of 1 m from the source. What will be the measured intensity 10 m away from the source, treating the source as point-like?

Unstable elementary particles are produced traveling at $v = 0.6c$ with respect to an observer in a laboratory. These particles have a typical intrinsic lifetime of 100 ns. In the frame of the observer, how long will the particles typically last before decaying?
A red beam of light is made up of a stream of photons. If we double the frequency of the beam, keeping the intensity constant, what will happen to the stream?

choices.a: The number of photons will stay the same and the energy of each photon will stay the same.
choices.b: The number of photons will stay the same and the energy of each photon will increase.
choices.c: The number of photons will decrease and the energy of each photon will increase.
choices.d: The number of photons will increase and the energy of each photon will stay the same.
choices.e: The number of photons will increase and the energy of each photon will increase.

questions.text: A beam of electrons is sent through a small hole in a piece of foil. The places where the electrons hit a distant screen are recorded. If we make the hole smaller, the region where the electrons are hitting the screen will be:

choices.d: there is not enough information to answer

questions.diagram_description: Shows electrons passing through a hole in foil and hitting a screen, with dots representing electron impact points on the screen

questions.text: The magnitude of the gravitational force with which a ping pong ball attracts the Earth is:

choices.a: equal to the magnitude of the gravitational force with which the Earth attracts that ping pong ball
choices.b: greater than the magnitude of the gravitational force with which the Earth attracts that ping pong ball
choices.c: less than the magnitude of the gravitational force with which the Earth attracts that ping pong ball

questions.text: An object is released from rest at a height $h$. For the object to go around the loop (of radius $r$) what is the smallest possible value of $h$?
questions.diagram_description: Shows a track with a circular loop of radius r, with an object starting from height h

questions.text: Two projectiles, K and Z, are launched from the top of a building with the same initial speed. Their launch angles (relative to the horizontal ground) are $\theta_K = 45°$ and $\theta_Z = -45°$. Which projectile will hit the ground with greater speed (neglecting air resistance)?

choices.c: Both will have the same speed
choices.d: K will never hit the ground
choices.e: There is not enough information to tell

questions.text: A ray of light passes through three media as shown in the figure. How are the speeds of light in these media related?
questions.diagram_description: Shows a light ray bending as it passes through three different media labeled 1, 2, and 3

questions.text: Two space heaters are made to output their nominal power of 1000W and 500W respectively when they are supplied with 120V. If they are connected in series to a normal (120V) wall outlet, compared to only the 1000W heater how much heat will be produced?

A magnet is held between two conductive rings that are attached to a frictionless rail by two insulators (They can move freely to the left and right). If we move the magnet to the left what will happen to the rings?

choices.a: A will move right and B will move left
choices.b: A will move left and B will move right

questions.diagram_description: Shows a bar magnet labeled N-S positioned between two conducting rings A and B on a rail system with insulators

questions.text: Consider two identical homogeneous balls, A and B, with the same initial temperatures. One of them is at rest on a horizontal plane, while the second one hangs on a thread. The same quantity of heat has been supplied to both balls. Which of the following statements is true about the temperatures of A and B?
questions.diagram_description: Shows two identical balls - ball B resting on a horizontal surface and ball A hanging from a thread

questions.text: A square hole is cut out from a rectangular sheet of metal. What would the shape of the hole be after heating the piece up? The pictures are focused on the hole and do not show the whole sheet.
questions.diagram_description: Shows a square hole in metal sheet at top, followed by four answer choices showing different hole shapes after heating

questions.text: The lens shown below constructs a real image (B) of the arrow (A) on the screen (S). What will happen to the image if we paint the upper half of the lens black?

choices.b: The upper half of the image will disappear
choices.c: The lower half of the image will disappear
choices.d: The shape of the image will remain the same and will only get dimmer

questions.diagram_description: Shows a converging lens with object arrow A on left, lens in middle, and inverted image B on screen S on right, with light rays traced through the system

questions.text: A ring is placed at a distance 2F on the left side of a lens of focal distance F, along the optical axis. Which of those pictures best shows the shape of the image?

choices.a: Diagram showing ring at 2F distance with upward arrow and image at 2F distance with downward arrow
choices.b: Diagram showing ring at 2F distance with upward arrow and larger image at 2F distance with downward arrow
choices.c: Diagram showing ring at 2F distance with upward arrow and elongated oval image at 2F distance with downward arrow
choices.d: Diagram showing ring at 2F distance with upward arrow and image at 2F distance with downward arrow, both same size

questions.diagram_description: Four diagrams (a-d) showing a lens with a ring object placed at distance 2F on the left side, and different representations of the resulting image at distance 2F on the right side. Each shows different image shapes and orientations.

questions.text: While on a long plane ride over the equator (where the Earth's radius is 6 378 km), the captain announces that the local gravitational acceleration is 9.75 m/s². At approximately what altitude is the plane flying?
